content:
A teacher's appointment is not merely a recruitment it's a solemn trust placed in the hands of those who will shape India's future. When a Teacher Eligibility Test is compromised through a multi-crore paper leak, the damage extends far beyond one examination; it undermines merit, betrays the aspirations of lakhs of honest candidates, and weakens public confidence in our education system. In my latest article, I examine the alleged ₹1.5 crore Maharashtra TET paper leak, the stringent legal consequences under the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024, the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, and the urgent responsibility of the Union and State Governments to secure public examinations through robust legal and technological safeguards. This is not just an education issueit is a constitutional, legal, and national concern demanding collective action from lawmakers, judges, administrators, teachers, parents, and citizens alike.
